titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L1000
GA L1000
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: 10.1 changed to 10.2 at M9D21b by Rachel Kevern.
Format of S1 W7 D7 changed by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 9.10 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
F 10v and 11r deleted, all tagging corrected accordingly by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 21.24-2 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Long addition at end of 17.17 changed to 17.11 and 17.12 by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost Pages removed by Catherine Smith.
Word break at 15.16 (first occurrence) corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 8.6 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L 1000Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rΒase text: TRnsΤranscribed by: MACΤranscription begun: 17 Oct 2013Τranscription finished: 14 Nov 2013 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: Folios 2r-5v are a supplement in a different hand. Images for folios 10v-11r are not present and so could not be transcribed. Misspellings, especially confusion of similar sounding vowels, are common. The folio numbering is incorrect in some places. The writing on many folios has been traced over to reinforce the fading ink. M1 D14b transcribed by acm.

div.note: The beginning of this lection (1.18ff.) is lacunose.

ab.note: Folios 2-5 are supplementary and written by a different hand.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday

ab.note: ο meant to be in margin but not visible.
θ meant to be in margin but not visible.
τ meant to be in margin but not visible.

div.note: The beginning of this lection (5.17) is lacunose.

ab.note: ε meant to be in margin but not visible.
ε meant to be in margin but not visible.

div.note: John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)

ab.note: ι meant to be in margin but not visible.

div.note: John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: A note to mark a new section is added: εις εγκαινια.

div.note: John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
This reading is normally found on S1 W7 D3.
This reading is normally found on S1 W7 D2.

ab.note: There is a marginal note here to mark the beginning of S4 W1 D6.

div.note: It is not clear which verse was originally written on this and the following lines so we have not speculated. It appears that a corrector erased these lines and began to add verse 15 in the available space, ran out of room and so began the correction afresh in the top margin, continuing verse 16 down the right margin. We have transcribed the marginal correction but not the overwriting.
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
The heading for this lection is on F92v: ευαγγελια τη αγια παρασκευη την λειτουργιαν.
The heading instructs the user to read this lection on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.

ab.note: Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.

div.note: John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
